text.title: Vector DB Dev Setup — Core Rules
text.summary: Five rules: pin image version, persistent volume, matching client version, smoke test, .gitignore the data dir.

rule.statement: Docker image MUST pin major.minor (qdrant/qdrant:v1.10.0 — not :latest). Floating tags break reproducibility.
rule.rationale: `:latest` changes silently; identical compose runs different software week-over-week. Pinned tag is the only reliable shareable env.

rule.statement: Container MUST mount a persistent host volume for data dir (e.g. `./qdrant_storage`). Ephemeral runs lose all data on restart.
rule.rationale: Without volume, every restart re-ingests; team wastes hours and forgets why.

rule.statement: Python client version MUST match server (e.g. qdrant-client 1.10.x for server 1.10.x). Cross-version pairings produce subtle protocol bugs.
rule.rationale: Each minor server bump may change wire format. Client and server pinned together avoid "search returns []" mysteries.

rule.statement: Dev setup MUST include a smoke script that creates a collection, inserts ≥10 vectors, runs a search, asserts non-empty result. New devs MUST run smoke before writing pipeline code.
rule.rationale: Smoke catches setup issues in minutes vs days spent debugging pipeline code that's actually fine.

rule.statement: Data dir (qdrant_storage, weaviate_data, etc.) MUST be in .gitignore. Committed binary data bloats history.
rule.rationale: Binary blobs in history are slow to clone, cannot be diffed. Always exclude data dirs.
